title: Lemon Herb Roasted Spatchcock Chicken
description: Spatchcocked chicken rubbed with lemon, garlic, rosemary, and thyme, oven-roasted at high heat until golden and crispy. Faster and more evenly cooked than a whole bird.

instructions.text: Spatchcock the chicken: place breast-side down, cut along both sides of the backbone with kitchen shears and remove it. Flip breast-side up and press firmly on the breastbone until it cracks flat. Pat completely dry with paper towels.
Mix olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, rosemary, thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper in a small bowl. Rub the mixture all over the chicken, including under the skin.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Place chicken on a baking sheet or in a roasting pan.
Roast 45–55 minutes until the skin is golden and crispy and an instant-read thermometer reads 165°F in the thickest part of the thigh.
Rest 10 minutes before carving. Do not skip the rest.

notes:
Getting the marinade under the skin directly onto the breast meat makes a significant difference in flavor -- use your fingers to loosen the skin and rub in as much as possible.

For extra-crispy skin, let the rubbed chicken sit uncovered in the fridge for 1–4 hours (or overnight) before roasting.

The pan drippings with lemon and herbs make an excellent quick pan sauce -- pour off most of the fat, add a splash of chicken stock, and scrape up the bits over medium heat.
